revision.text:
'''大山寺'''（おおやまでら、だいせんじ、たいさんじ）は[[仏教]]の[[寺院]]で各地に存在する。
* [[神奈川県]][[伊勢原市]]にある[[真言宗]]の寺院で山号は雨降山。通称は'''大山不動'''。詳しくは別項[[大山寺 (伊勢原市)]]を参照。 * [[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[大山町 (鳥取県)|大山町]]にある[[天台宗]]の寺院で山号は角磐山。詳しくは別項[[大山寺 (鳥取県西伯郡大山町)]]を参照。 * [[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]にある[[天台宗]]の寺院、'''太山寺'''の旧寺号。[[元正天皇]]の勅願寺。詳しくは別項[[太山寺 (神戸市)]]を参照。
{{aimai}} [[Category:曖昧さ回避|おおやまてら]] [[Category:曖昧さ回避|たいせんし]]

==地名== * 愛知県岩倉市の地名。[[大山寺駅]]
{{aimai}}

'''大山寺'''（おおやまでら、だいせんじ、たいさんじ）は、[[仏教]]の[[寺院]]。日本各地に存在する。
== 寺院 == * [[大山寺 (栃木市)]] - [[栃木県]][[栃木市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (伊勢原市)]] - [[神奈川県]][[伊勢原市]]にある[[真言宗大覚寺派]]の寺院。通称：大山不動。 * [[大山寺 (岩倉市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岩倉市]]にある寺院。 * [[太山寺 (神戸市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。旧寺号が大山寺。[[元正天皇]]の勅願寺。 * [[大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)]] - [[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[大山町]]にある天台宗の寺院。 * [[大山寺 (尾道市)]] - [[広島県]][[尾道市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (徳島県上板町)]] - [[徳島県]][[板野郡]][[上板町]]にある寺院。[[四国別格二十霊場]]1番札所。 * [[大山寺 (大分市)]] - [[大分県]][[大分市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (指宿市)]] - [[鹿児島県]][[指宿市]]にある寺院。
== その他 == * [[大山寺町]] - 愛知県岩倉市の地名。 * [[大山寺駅]] - 愛知県岩倉市大山寺町にある、[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]]の[[鉄道駅|駅]]。
{{aimai}} [[Category:同名の寺|たいせんし]]

'''大山寺'''（おおやまでら、だいせんじ、たいさんじ）は、[[仏教]]の[[寺院]]。日本各地に存在する。
== 寺院 == * [[大山寺 (栃木市)]] - [[栃木県]][[栃木市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (伊勢原市)]] - [[神奈川県]][[伊勢原市]]にある[[真言宗大覚寺派]]の寺院。通称：大山不動。 * [[大山寺 (岩倉市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岩倉市]]にある寺院。 * [[太山寺 (神戸市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。旧寺号が大山寺。[[元正天皇]]の勅願寺。 * [[大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)]] - [[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[大山町]]にある天台宗の寺院。 * [[大山寺 (尾道市)]] - [[広島県]][[尾道市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (徳島県上板町)]] - [[徳島県]][[板野郡]][[上板町]]にある寺院。[[四国別格二十霊場]]1番札所。 * [[大山寺 (大分市)]] - [[大分県]][[大分市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (指宿市)]] - [[鹿児島県]][[指宿市]]にある寺院。
== 鉄道駅 == * [[大山寺駅 (神奈川県)]] （おおやまでらえき） - 神奈川県伊勢原市大山にある、[[大山観光電鉄]][[大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線|大山鋼索線]]の[[鉄道駅|駅]]。 * [[大山寺駅 (愛知県)]] （たいさんじえき） - 愛知県岩倉市大山寺町にある、[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]]の駅。
== その他 == * [[大山寺町]] - 愛知県岩倉市の地名。
{{aimai}} [[Category:同名の寺|たいせんし]]

revision.text:
'''大山寺'''（おおやまでら、おおやまじ、だいせんじ、たいさんじ）は、[[仏教]]の[[寺院]]。日本各地に存在する。
== 寺院 == * [[大山寺 (栃木市)]] - [[栃木県]][[栃木市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (鴨川市)]] - [[千葉県]][[鴨川市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (伊勢原市)]] - [[神奈川県]][[伊勢原市]]にある[[真言宗大覚寺派]]の寺院。通称：大山不動。 * [[大山寺 (岩倉市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岩倉市]]にある寺院。 * [[太山寺 (神戸市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。旧寺号が大山寺。[[元正天皇]]の勅願寺。 * [[大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)]] - [[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[大山町]]にある天台宗の寺院。 * [[大山寺 (尾道市)]] - [[広島県]][[尾道市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (徳島県上板町)]] - [[徳島県]][[板野郡]][[上板町]]にある寺院。[[四国別格二十霊場]]1番札所。 * [[大山寺 (大分市)]] - [[大分県]][[大分市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (指宿市)]] - [[鹿児島県]][[指宿市]]にある寺院。
== 鉄道駅 == * [[大山寺駅 (神奈川県)]] （おおやまでらえき） - 神奈川県伊勢原市大山にある、[[大山観光電鉄]][[大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線|大山鋼索線]]の[[鉄道駅|駅]]。 * [[大山寺駅 (愛知県)]] （たいさんじえき） - 愛知県岩倉市大山寺町にある、[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]]の駅。
== その他 == * [[大山寺町]] - 愛知県岩倉市の地名。
{{aimai}} [[Category:同名の寺|たいせんし]]

'''大山寺'''（おおやまでら、おおやまじ、だいせんじ、たいさんじ）は、[[仏教]]の[[寺院]]。日本各地に存在する。
== 寺院 == * [[大山寺 (茨城県城里町)]] * [[大山寺 (栃木市)]] - [[栃木県]][[栃木市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (鴨川市)]] - [[千葉県]][[鴨川市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (伊勢原市)]] - [[神奈川県]][[伊勢原市]]にある[[真言宗大覚寺派]]の寺院。通称：大山不動。 * [[大山寺 (岩倉市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岩倉市]]にある寺院。 * [[太山寺 (神戸市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。旧寺号が大山寺。[[元正天皇]]の勅願寺。 * [[大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)]] - [[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[大山町]]にある天台宗の寺院。 * [[大山寺 (尾道市)]] - [[広島県]][[尾道市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (徳島県上板町)]] - [[徳島県]][[板野郡]][[上板町]]にある寺院。[[四国別格二十霊場]]1番札所。 * [[大山寺 (大分市)]] - [[大分県]][[大分市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (指宿市)]] - [[鹿児島県]][[指宿市]]にある寺院。
== 鉄道駅 == * [[大山寺駅 (神奈川県)]] （おおやまでらえき） - 神奈川県伊勢原市大山にある、[[大山観光電鉄]][[大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線|大山鋼索線]]の[[鉄道駅|駅]]。 * [[大山寺駅 (愛知県)]] （たいさんじえき） - 愛知県岩倉市大山寺町にある、[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]]の駅。
== その他 == * [[大山寺町]] - 愛知県岩倉市の地名。
{{aimai}} [[Category:同名の寺|たいせんし]]

revision.text:
'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
{{aimai}} [[Category:Temples with identical names|Ōyama-dera]]

revision.text:
'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
{{aimai}} [[Category:Temples with identical names|Ōyama-dera]] [[Category:大山寺]][[ja:大山寺]]

The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.

revision.comment: /* 寺院 */内容追加。
revision.text:
'''大山寺'''（おおやまでら、おおやまじ、だいせんじ、たいさんじ）は、[[仏教]]の[[寺院]]。日本各地に存在する。
== 寺院 == * [[大山寺 (茨城県城里町)]] - [[茨城県]][[東茨城郡]][[城里町]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (栃木市)]] - [[栃木県]][[栃木市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (鴨川市)]] - [[千葉県]][[鴨川市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (伊勢原市)]] - [[神奈川県]][[伊勢原市]]にある[[真言宗大覚寺派]]の寺院。通称：大山不動。 * [[大山寺 (岩倉市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岩倉市]]にある寺院。 * [[太山寺 (神戸市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。旧寺号が大山寺。[[元正天皇]]の勅願寺。 * [[大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)]] - [[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[大山町]]にある天台宗の寺院。 * [[大山寺 (尾道市)]] - [[広島県]][[尾道市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (徳島県上板町)]] - [[徳島県]][[板野郡]][[上板町]]にある寺院。[[四国別格二十霊場]]1番札所。 * [[大山寺 (大分市)]] - [[大分県]][[大分市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (指宿市)]] - [[鹿児島県]][[指宿市]]にある寺院。
== 鉄道駅 == * [[大山寺駅 (神奈川県)]] （おおやまでらえき） - 神奈川県伊勢原市大山にある、[[大山観光電鉄]][[大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線|大山鋼索線]]の[[鉄道駅|駅]]。 * [[大山寺駅 (愛知県)]] （たいさんじえき） - 愛知県岩倉市大山寺町にある、[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]]の駅。
== その他 == * [[大山寺町]] - 愛知県岩倉市の地名。
{{aimai}} [[Category:同名の寺|たいせんし]]

revision.text:
'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
{{aimai}} [[Category:Temples with identical names|Ōyama-dera]] [[Category:大山寺]][[ja:大山寺]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{afc comment|1=trimming down from {{ill|Temple and Shrine Authorities|ja|寺社勢力}} so the content will be smaller when completed and of a different nature. ChatGPT please grab the Japanese after {{ill|{{subst:PAGENAME}}|ja|... and place it here like [[ja:this]] ~~~~}} '''Jisha Seiryoku''' (寺社勢力) refers to the religious and temple powers of Japan, also known as '''Shaji Seiryoku''' (社寺勢力).<ref>[[Hiraizumi Kiyomi]] used the term "Shaji" and "Shaji..."

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] to [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT{{subst:PAGENAME}}}}[[category:fix the O]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
{{aimai}} [[Category:Temples with identical names|Ōyama-dera]] [[Category:大山寺]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ōyamadera]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTŌyamadera}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Ōyamadera]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTŌyamadera}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:大山寺]]

'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.

Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
{{aimai}} [[Category:Temples with identical names|Ōyama-dera]] [[Category:大山寺]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ōyamadera]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oyamadera}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Ōyamadera]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oyamadera}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:大山寺]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing pages to myself
revision.text:
'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, おおやまでら, おおやまじ, だいせんじ, たいさんじ) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisanji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[Ōyama Cable Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisanji Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.

'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, おおやまでら, おおやまじ, だいせんじ, たいさんじ) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisanji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].

'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, おおやまでら, おおやまじ, だいせんじ, たいさんじ) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisanji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[Ōyama Cable Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisanji Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
{{aimai}} [[Category:Temples with identical names]] [[ja:大山寺]]

revision.text:
'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, おおやまでら, おおやまじ, だいせんじ, たいさんじ) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisanji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[Ōyama Cable Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisanji Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, おおやまでら, おおやまじ, だいせんじ, たいさんじ) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisanji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[Ōyama Cable Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisanji Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
{{aimai}} [[Category:Temples with identical names]] [[ja:大山寺]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
{{aimai}} [[Category:Temples with identical names|Ōyama-dera]] [[Category:大山寺]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ōyamadera]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Oyamadera}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Ōyamadera]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:Ōyamadera]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Oyamadera}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Ōyamadera]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:大山寺]]

'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.

revision.text:
'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, おおやまでら, おおやまじ, だいせんじ, たいさんじ) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisanji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[Ōyama Cable Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisanji Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisanji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[Ōyama Cable Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisanji Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. '''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|:en:Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== See Also ==

revision.text:
'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.

'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|:en:Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== See Also ==

'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|:en:Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.

A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.

revision.comment: tagging the shrine
revision.text:
'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|:en:Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== See Also ==

Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|:en:Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== See Also ==

revision.text:
'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|:en:Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== See Also ==

revision.text:
'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
