'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|:en:Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.

'''Ōyama-dera''' (大山寺, also read as Ōyama-ji, Daisen-ji, or Taisan-ji) refers to multiple {{ill|Buddhist temples|ja|仏教寺院}} across Japan.
== Temples == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - Located in Shirosato, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple in Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple in Kamogawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daikakuji School|ja|真言宗大覚寺派}} temple, also known as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A temple in Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Formerly known as Ōyama-dera, it is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple and an imperial prayer temple of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Daisen, Tottori)|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple in Daisen Town, Saihaku District, Tottori Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - Located in Onomichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Kamiita, Tokushima)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple in Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture. The first temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Betsugaku Nijū Reijō|ja|四国別格二十霊場}} pilgrimage. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple in Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - Located in Ibusuki City, Kagoshima Prefecture.
== Train Stations == * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)}} (Ōyama-dera Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} in Ōyama, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōyama-dera Station (Aichi)|ja|大山寺駅 (愛知県)}} (Taisan-ji Eki) - A station on the {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}} in Ōyamadera-chō, Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.
== Other == * {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A neighborhood in Iwakura City, Aichi Prefecture.

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|:en:Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== See Also ==

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Ōyama-dera]] to [[Ōyamadera]]
revision.text:
'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|:en:Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== See Also ==
* [[Oyama Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] * [[:en:大山|大山]] ** [[Daisen (disambiguation)]] ** [[Ōyama (disambiguation)]] {{aimai}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[ja:大山寺]] '''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. revision.text:
'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|:en:Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== See Also ==
* [[Oyama Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] * [[:en:大山|大山]] ** [[Daisen (disambiguation)]] ** [[Ōyama (disambiguation)]] {{aimai}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[ja:大山寺]] '''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|:en:Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== See Also ==

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. '''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==

== Other ==

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture. == See Also ==

'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture. == See Also ==
* [[Oyama Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] * [[:en:大山|大山]] ** [[Daisen (disambiguation)]] ** [[Ōyama (disambiguation)]] {{aimai}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[ja:大山寺]] revision.text:
'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture. == See Also ==
* [[Oyama Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] * [[:en:大山|大山]] ** [[Daisen (disambiguation)]] ** [[Ōyama (disambiguation)]] {{aimai}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[ja:大山寺]] revision.text:
'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture. == See Also ==
* [[Oyama Shrines]] * [[:en:大山|大山]] ** [[Daisen (disambiguation)]] ** [[Ōyama (disambiguation)]] {{aimai}}
*
<!--Ōyama-dera-->
<!--Ōyama-dera-->

revision.text:
'''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)|12=draft}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)|12=draft}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)|12=draft}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|12=draft}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町|12=draft}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture. == See Also ==
* [[Oyama Shrines]] * [[:en:大山|大山]] ** [[Daisen (disambiguation)]] ** [[Ōyama (disambiguation)]] {{aimai}}
*

* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)|12=draft}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|ja|太山寺 (神戸市)|12=draft}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * {{ill|Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)|12=draft}} - A Tendai sect temple located in [[Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|ja|大山寺 (徳島県上板町)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)|12=draft}} - A temple located in [[Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|12=draft}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町|12=draft}} - A district in [[Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture. == See Also ==
* [[Oyama Shrines]] * [[:en:大山|大山]] ** [[Daisen (disambiguation)]] ** [[Ōyama (disambiguation)]] {{aimai}}
*

revision.comment: 27 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大山寺 up to rev 103276406
revision.text:
'''大山寺'''（おおやまでら、おおやまじ、だいせんじ、たいさんじ）は、[[仏教]]の[[寺院]]。日本各地に存在する。
== 寺院 == * [[大山寺 (茨城県城里町)]] - [[茨城県]][[東茨城郡]][[城里町]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (栃木市)]] - [[栃木県]][[栃木市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (鴨川市)]] - [[千葉県]][[鴨川市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (伊勢原市)]] - [[神奈川県]][[伊勢原市]]にある[[真言宗大覚寺派]]の寺院。通称：大山不動。 * [[大山寺 (岩倉市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岩倉市]]にある寺院。 * [[太山寺 (神戸市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。旧寺号が大山寺。[[元正天皇]]の勅願寺。 * [[大山寺 (鳥取県大山町)]] - [[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[大山町]]にある天台宗の寺院。 * [[大山寺 (尾道市)]] - [[広島県]][[尾道市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (徳島県上板町)]] - [[徳島県]][[板野郡]][[上板町]]にある寺院。[[四国別格二十霊場]]1番札所。 * [[大山寺 (大分市)]] - [[大分県]][[大分市]]にある寺院。 * [[大山寺 (指宿市)]] - [[鹿児島県]][[指宿市]]にある寺院。
== 鉄道駅 == * [[大山寺駅 (神奈川県)]] （おおやまでらえき） - 神奈川県伊勢原市大山にある、[[大山観光電鉄]][[大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線|大山鋼索線]]の[[鉄道駅|駅]]。 * [[大山寺駅 (愛知県)]] （たいさんじえき） - 愛知県岩倉市大山寺町にある、[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]]の駅。
== その他 == * [[大山寺町]] - 愛知県岩倉市の地名。
{{aimai}} [[Category:同名の寺|たいせんし]]

== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|qq=}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[:en:Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|qq=}} - A temple located in [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|qq=}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[:en:Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kobe)|Taisan-ji (Kobe)]] - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * [[:en:Daisen-ji|Daisen-ji]] - A Tendai sect temple located in [[:en:Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|qq=}} - A temple located in [[Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)]] - A temple located in [[:en:Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[:en:Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ōita)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].

* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|qq=}} - A temple located in [[Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[:en:Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|qq=}} - A temple located in [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|qq=}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[:en:Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kobe)|Taisan-ji (Kobe)]] - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[:en:Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * [[:en:Daisen-ji|Daisen-ji]] - A Tendai sect temple located in [[:en:Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|qq=}} - A temple located in [[:en:Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)]] - A temple located in [[:en:Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[:en:Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ōita)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Cable Car|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[:en:Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera-chō|qq=}} - A district in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture. == See Also ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11434608]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[:en:Temple|Temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|qq=}} - A temple located in [[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[:en:Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|qq=|1=Ōyama-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683161}} - A temple located in [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|qq=|1=Ōyama-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434610}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[:en:Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kobe)|Taisan-ji (Kobe)]] - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[:en:Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * [[:en:Daisen-ji|Daisen-ji]] - A Tendai sect temple located in [[:en:Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|qq=|1=Taisan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29116035}} - A temple located in [[:en:Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)]] - A temple located in [[:en:Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[:en:Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|qq=|1=Pilgrimage of the 20 exceptional temples of Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419707}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ōita)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Cable Car|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線|1=Ōyama Cable Car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q864604}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[:en:Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11434608]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[:en:Temple|Temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|qq=}} - A temple located in [[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[:en:Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|qq=|1=Ōyama-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683161}} - A temple located in [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|qq=|1=Ōyama-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434610}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[:en:Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kobe)|Taisan-ji (Kobe)]] - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[:en:Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * [[:en:Daisen-ji|Daisen-ji]] - A Tendai sect temple located in [[:en:Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|qq=|1=Taisan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29116035}} - A temple located in [[:en:Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)]] - A temple located in [[:en:Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[:en:Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|qq=|1=Pilgrimage of the 20 exceptional temples of Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419707}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ōita)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Cable Car|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線|1=Ōyama Cable Car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q864604}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[:en:Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera-chō|qq=|1=Ōyamadera-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044253}} - A district in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture. == See Also ==

Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|qq=}} - A temple located in [[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[:en:Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|1=Ōyama-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683161}} - A temple located in [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|1=Ōyama-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434610}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[:en:Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kobe)|Taisan-ji (Kobe)]] - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[:en:Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * [[:en:Daisen-ji|Daisen-ji]] - A Tendai sect temple located in [[:en:Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|1=Taisan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29116035}} - A temple located in [[:en:Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)]] - A temple located in [[:en:Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[:en:Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|1=Pilgrimage of the 20 exceptional temples of Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419707}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山寺]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11434608]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[:en:Temple|Temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|qq=}} - A temple located in [[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[:en:Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|1=Ōyama-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683161}} - A temple located in [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|1=Ōyama-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434610}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[:en:Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kobe)|Taisan-ji (Kobe)]] - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[:en:Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * [[:en:Daisen-ji|Daisen-ji]] - A Tendai sect temple located in [[:en:Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|1=Taisan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29116035}} - A temple located in [[:en:Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)]] - A temple located in [[:en:Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[:en:Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|1=Pilgrimage of the 20 exceptional temples of Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419707}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Cable Car|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線|1=Ōyama Cable Car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q864604}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[:en:Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera-chō|1=Ōyamadera-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044253}} - A district in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture. == See Also ==
* [[Oyama Shrines]] * [[:en:大山|大山]] ** [[:en:Daisen (disambiguation)|Daisen (disambiguation)]] ** [[:en:Ōyama (disambiguation)|Ōyama (disambiguation)]] {{disambiguation}}
*

It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * [[:en:Daisen-ji|Daisen-ji]] - A Tendai sect temple located in [[:en:Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|1=Taisan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29116035}} - A temple located in [[:en:Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)]] - A temple located in [[:en:Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[:en:Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|1=Pilgrimage of the 20 exceptional temples of Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419707}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山寺]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11434608]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[:en:Temple|Temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|qq=}} - A temple located in [[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[:en:Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|1=Ōyama-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683161}} - A temple located in [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|1=Ōyama-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434610}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[:en:Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kobe)|Taisan-ji (Kobe)]] - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[:en:Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * [[:en:Daisen-ji|Daisen-ji]] - A Tendai sect temple located in [[:en:Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|1=Taisan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29116035}} - A temple located in [[:en:Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)]] - A temple located in [[:en:Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[:en:Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|1=Pilgrimage of the 20 exceptional temples of Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419707}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Cable Car|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線|1=Ōyama Cable Car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q864604}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[:en:Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山寺]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11434608]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[:en:Temple|Temple]]. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.
== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|qq=}} - A temple located in [[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[:en:Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|1=Ōyama-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683161}} - A temple located in [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|1=Ōyama-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434610}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[:en:Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kobe)|Taisan-ji (Kobe)]] - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[:en:Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * [[:en:Daisen-ji|Daisen-ji]] - A Tendai sect temple located in [[:en:Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|1=Taisan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29116035}} - A temple located in [[:en:Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)]] - A temple located in [[:en:Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[:en:Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|1=Pilgrimage of the 20 exceptional temples of Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419707}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Cable Car|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線|1=Ōyama Cable Car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q864604}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[:en:Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera-chō|ja|大山寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera-chō|1=Ōyamadera-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044253}} - A district in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture. == See Also ==
* [[Oyama Shrines]] * [[:en:大山|大山]] ** [[:en:Daisen (disambiguation)|Daisen (disambiguation)]] ** [[:en:Ōyama (disambiguation)|Ōyama (disambiguation)]] {{disambiguation}}

Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kobe)|Taisan-ji (Kobe)]] - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[:en:Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * [[:en:Daisen-ji|Daisen-ji]] - A Tendai sect temple located in [[:en:Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|1=Taisan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29116035}} - A temple located in [[:en:Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)]] - A temple located in [[:en:Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[:en:Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|1=Pilgrimage of the 20 exceptional temples of Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419707}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].

The first stop of the {{ill|Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|ja|四国別格二十霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty Sacred Places Outside Shikoku|1=Pilgrimage of the 20 exceptional temples of Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419707}}. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja|大山寺 (大分市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ōita)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ōita, Ōita|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja|大山寺 (指宿市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Ibusuki, Kagoshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Ibusuki, Kagoshima|Ibusuki City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Railway Stations ==
* {{ill|Ōyama Cable Car|ja|大山寺駅 (神奈川県)|Ōyama Cable Car=Ōyama Cable Car|lt=Ōyamadera Station (Kanagawa)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Cable Car|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山観光電鉄大山鋼索線|1=Ōyama Cable Car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q864604}} (おおやまでらえき) - A station on the [[:en:Ōyama_Cable_Car|Ōyama Cable Line]] operated by the [[Ōyama Tourism Railway]], located in [[Ōyama, Isehara, Kanagawa|Ōyama]], [[Isehara City]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * [[:en:Taisanji_Station|Taisanji Station]] (たいさんじえき) - A station on the [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] operated by [[:en:Nagoya Railroad|Meitetsu]], located in [[:en:Iwakura, Aichi|Iwakura City]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Other ==

== Temples ==
* {{ill|Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|ja|大山寺 (茨城県城里町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Shirosato, Ibaraki)|qq=}} - A temple located in [[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato]], [[:en:Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|Higashiibaraki District]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja|大山寺 (栃木市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Tochigi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in [[:en:Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|ja|大山寺 (鴨川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Kamogawa, Chiba)|1=Ōyama-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683161}} - A temple located in [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|ja|大山寺 (伊勢原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Isehara, Kanagawa)|1=Ōyama-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434610}} - A [[Shingon Daikakuji School|Shingon Daikakuji sect]] temple located in [[:en:Isehara, Kanagawa|Isehara City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. Former temple name was Ōyamadera. It was an imperial prayer temple of Empress Genmei. * [[:en:Daisen-ji|Daisen-ji]] - A Tendai sect temple located in [[:en:Daisen, Tottori|Daisen Town]], [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saihaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|ja|大山寺 (尾道市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Onomichi, Hiroshima)|1=Taisan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29116035}} - A temple located in [[:en:Onomichi, Hiroshima|Onomichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Taisan-ji (Kamiita)|Taisan-ji (Kamiita)]] - A temple located in [[:en:Kamiita, Tokushima|Kamiita Town]], [[:en:Itano District, Tokushima|Itano District]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. '''Ōyamadera''' (大山寺, also read as Oyamaji, Daisenji, and Taisanji) is a {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhist|1=Buddhism}} {{ill|Temple|en|Temple|WD=Q44539|lt=Temple|1=Temple}}. Temples with this name exist in various locations across Japan.

Commonly referred to as Ōyama Fudō. * {{ill|Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja|大山寺 (岩倉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamadera (Iwakura, Aichi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A temple located in {{ill|Iwakura, Aichi|en|Iwakura, Aichi|WD=Q648275|lt=Iwakura City|1=Iwakura, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|en|Taisan-ji (Kobe)|WD=Q3514065|lt=Taisan-ji (Kobe)|1=Taisan-ji (Kobe)}} - A [[Tendai sect|Tendai]] temple located in [[:en:Kobe, Hyōgo|Kobe City]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}.
